101: **France**, top of **Group I** with 9 points from three wins and a goal difference of +8, face **Spain**, first in **Group H** on 7 points with a GD of +5, in the first semifinal at AT&T Stadium in Arlington (12:00 PM PT). **France** beat Morocco 2-0 in the quarter-final — Mbappé scoring his eighth goal of the tournament to reach 20 career finals goals, one behind Messi's all-time record, before being withdrawn at 77 minutes with an ankle knock he has since described as minor — while Deschamps sweats on the fitness of Tchouaméni (adductor) and Koné (knee). **Spain** needed an 88th-minute Mikel Merino substitute winner to beat Belgium 2-1, with Merino becoming the first player in tournament history to score the winning goal as a substitute in two different knockout matches; the victors face the England vs Argentina winner on July 19 in New Jersey.
